title: 10 best herbal teas for jaw clenching
intro_desc: Herbal teas for jaw clenching are natural remedies made from various plants and herbs that help alleviate the symptoms of teeth grinding and jaw clenching, also known as bruxism. These teas often contain soothing ingredients like chamomile, valerian root, and lemon balm, which promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension. By drinking these teas before bedtime, individuals can prevent jaw clenching and teeth grinding during sleep, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced jaw pain. Furthermore, regular consumption of herbal teas can also help manage stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for bruxism. As a result, many people have reported significant improvements in their overall well-being and quality of life after incorporating herbal teas into their daily routine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with jaw clenching because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax the muscles in the face and jaw. The soothing properties of chamomile can also reduce inflammation and tension in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which is often a source of jaw pain and clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, herbal German chamomile teas can provide relief from jaw clenching and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: German Chamomile tea helps to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to jaw clenching.

Muscle relaxant properties: The apigenin and other flavonoids in German Chamomile tea have been shown to relax muscles, including those in the face and jaw, which can help reduce tension and alleviate jaw clenching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: German Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help soothe and reduce inflammation in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to jaw pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in apigenin, an flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to alleviate jaw pain and tension.
Leaves: The leaves contain a high concentration of bisabolol, a sesquiterpene lactone that has been shown to reduce muscle spasms and ease tension in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried german chamomile flowers in bulk from a reputable supplier weighing approximately 20 grams.
Measure one teaspoon of the dried flower mixture and place it into a teapot or infuser for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat filtered water to a rolling boil and pour it over the herbal mixture allowing for a four-minute steeping time.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discarding any remaining solids if desired.
Serve the tea hot or allow it to cool to room temperature consuming within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with jaw clenching because it contains a compound called rosmarinic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help relax tense muscles. When consumed as a tea, sage's flavonoids and terpenes work together to reduce inflammation in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), the area where the jawbone and skull meet. As the inflammation subsides, the muscles surrounding the TMJ begin to relax, releasing the clenched jaw and alleviating tension headaches, migraines, and facial pain associated with chronic jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Sage tea helps relax tense muscles, including those in the jaw, reducing spasms and alleviating tension headaches and migraines associated with bruxism.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation in the gums, teeth, and jaw joints, which can contribute to clenching and grinding behaviors.

Cognitive benefits: Sage tea may improve cognitive function, particularly memory and focus, which can help individuals become more aware of their habits and better manage stress, leading to reduced jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw caused by clenching.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes contain compounds that can help relax the muscles in the jaw, reducing tension and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sage leaves from your garden or store-bought source and measure out two teaspoons of leaf material for each eight-ounce serving.
Combine the measured sage leaves with one quart of boiling water in a large saucepan and allow it to steep for five minutes to release the fragrant oils.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a heat-resistant container to separate the solids from the liquid.
Discard the solids and add honey or sugar to taste, if desired, then store the cooled tea in an airtight container for up to three days.
Serve the sage tea hot or chilled, garnished with a sprig of fresh sage leaves if desired, and enjoy its calming and cognitive-boosting properties within 30 minutes after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with jaw clenching because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax the muscles in the face and neck that are responsible for clenched jaws. The tea's soothing properties can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, lemon balm tea may be able to help alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, migraines, and other conditions characterized by jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tea helps to relax the muscles of the jaw, reducing spasms and tension that can lead to clenching.
Anxiolytic properties: The calming effects of lemon balm tea can help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for jaw clenching.
Sedative properties: Lemon balm tea's sedative properties can promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension, helping to release the clenched jaw.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm are valued for their calming properties, which help to soothe and relax the muscles in the jaw.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and tension in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lemon balm leaves from melissa officinalis plants in early morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Rinse 2-3 tablespoons of dried or 4-6 teaspoons of fresh lemon balm leaves with cold filtered water to remove any impurities.
Steep the rinsed lemon balm leaves in 8 oz of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the herbal oils and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or cheesecloth to remove the solids and enjoy the soothing herbal infusion.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, savoring the subtle citrus flavor and potential therapeutic benefits of lemon balm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with jaw clenching because its unique blend of herbs, particularly passionflower, calms the nervous system and relaxes the muscles in the face and neck. This reduction in tension allows for a decrease in teeth grinding and jaw clenching, providing relief from stress-related discomfort. The soothing properties of maypop also help to calm the mind and body, promoting overall relaxation and reducing symptoms of anxiety and insomnia that can contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Passionflower (Passiflora incarnata): Contains flavonoids and alkaloids that act as sedatives, helping to calm muscle spasms and reduce tension in the jaw, alleviating jaw clenching.
Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla): Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated jaw muscles and joints associated with bruxism (jaw clenching).

Lemon Balm (Melissa officinalis): Has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to jaw clenching, as well as providing a relaxing influence on facial muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, which can help relax tense muscles and ease jaw clenching.
Bark: Containing compounds like salicin and tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with jaw tension.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried or fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves.
Boil two cups of water in a medium-sized pot for five minutes.
Add the gathered herb to the boiling water and reduce heat to simmer for ten minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional three to five minutes before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap teas helps with jaw clenching because of its natural relaxing properties that target the muscles of the face, neck, and jaw. The tea's active compounds, such as baicalin and scutellarein, work to calm tense facial muscles, reducing the frequency and intensity of teeth grinding and jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, skullcap teas can help alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, migraines, and other conditions related to excessive jaw activity.
remedy_properties: Baicalin: Skullcap contains baicalin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.

Scutellarein: Scutellarein is another flavonoid found in skullcap that has been demonstrated to have sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension, including that of the jaw.

Volatile oils: Skullcap teas contain volatile oils such as beta-caryophyllene and germacrene B, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to soothe and relax the muscles in the face and jaw.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a rich concentration of sesquiterpenes, skullcap roots are used in teas to soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing tension-related jaw clenching.
Bark: The bark of the skullcap plant is rich in flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension, making it effective for relieving jaw clenching associated with stress.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried scutellaria lateriflora root per cup of boiling water.
Steep the skullcap tea for five to seven minutes in a tea infuser or strainer.
Strain the liquid from the solids and discard the spent herbs immediately.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it straight or adding sweeteners.
Consume one to two cups of herbal skullcap tea per day as needed for relaxation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with jaw clenching because of its calming properties, which can effectively relax the muscles in the face and neck. The tea's soothing essence can ease tension and stress, common contributors to jaw clenching, allowing for a more relaxed and open mouth position. Additionally, the herbal tea's ability to promote better sleep may also help alleviate morning stiffness and soreness associated with bruxism, further reducing the occurrence of jaw clenching throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Lavender tea's anxiolytic properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching, by promoting relaxation and calming the mind.
Muscle relaxant properties: The tea's muscle relaxant effects can directly target the jaw muscles, helping to relieve tension and spasms that contribute to jaw clenching.

Sedative properties: Lavender tea's sedative properties can help promote a sense of calmness and reduce irritability, which can lead to reduced jaw clenching due to decreased stress and emotional reactivity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calming compounds, lavender roots help to soothe and relax the muscles responsible for jaw tension.
Seeds: Small but potent, lavender seeds contain anti-inflammatory properties that can ease pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a medium-sized pot and then reduce heat to simmer.
Add the measured dried lavender flowers to the simmering water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or tea strainer to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming, enjoying warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime teas helps with jaw clenching because it contains a unique blend of herbs that work together to relax the muscles in the face, neck, and jaw. The calming properties of these herbs help to reduce tension and stiffness, allowing the jaw to release its grip on the teeth and return to a more natural state. This can provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with clenching and grinding, promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility in the process.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Large-leaved lime tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the jaw muscles, alleviating tension and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.

Muscle relaxant properties: The tea's flavonoids and saponins have been shown to possess muscle-relaxing properties, helping to soothe and calm tense jaw muscles, reducing the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes.

Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Large-leaved lime tea has been traditionally used as a natural stress reliever, which can help to reduce anxiety and tension that may contribute to jaw clenching, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation in the face and neck.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their calming properties, roots can help soothe jaw tension and reduce stress that may be contributing to clenching.
Bark: Containing natural anti-inflammatory compounds, bark can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves in abundance during the summer months for optimal flavor.
Rinse the collected leaves gently with clean water to remove any dirt or debris thoroughly.
Chop the rinsed leaves coarsely using a sharp knife or herb chopper into small fragments swiftly.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the chopped tilia platyphyllos leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes carefully.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaf particles and enjoy immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common lime teas helps with jaw clenching because of its calming properties, which can soothe tense muscles in the face and jaw. The herbal tea's ability to relax the body and mind can help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of jaw clenching. Additionally, the tea's citrus flavor can stimulate saliva production, which can help loosen up stiff joints and reduce muscle tension, providing relief from jaw clenching and related pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in common lime tea, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to relax the muscles of the face and jaw, reducing spasms and tension that can contribute to jaw clenching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in common lime tea, such as limonene and citral, may help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the jaw joints and surrounding tissues, which can exacerbate jaw clenching and other temporomandibular disorders (TMD).

Calming effects: The calming and soothing properties of common lime tea, particularly the flavonoids and essential oils, may help to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are common triggers for jaw clenching and bruxism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with jaw clenching.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the lime plant contain compounds that help relax muscles and ease tension, which can be beneficial in relieving symptoms of jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried tilia europaea flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat filtered water in a tea kettle or pot and let it reach a rolling boil.
Pour the boiling water over the measured tilia europaea flowers in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes depending on desired strength of tea.
Strain the tea into cups using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with jaw clenching because of its calming effect on the nervous system. The herb's sedative properties help to relax tense muscles in the face, neck, and shoulders, which can become strained when clenched. By soothing these areas, valerian tea reduces tension that may be contributing to jaw clenching and bruxism, allowing for a more relaxed and comfortable chewing experience. As a result, the tea promotes a sense of calm and tranquility, making it an effective natural remedy for managing chronic teeth grinding and jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian tea helps to relax the muscles in the face and jaw, reducing tension and clenching by promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's muscle-relaxing compounds help to ease spasms and contractions in the jaw muscles, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with teeth grinding or clenching.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Valerian tea has been shown to reduce anxiety levels, which can often contribute to jaw clenching and teeth grinding by reducing feelings of stress and tension in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are used because they contain the highest concentration of valepotriates, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and help relieve tension and pain in the jaw.

Bark: The bark of the valerian plant is used to make teas for jaw clenching due to its high content of alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with one quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the root mixture for 20-30 minutes, allowing it to infuse and release its active compounds.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours, or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus teas helps with jaw clenching because it contains a unique compound called lupulone, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. This soothing action can help to reduce tension in the jaw muscles and alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. As the tea works to calm the nerves, it also helps to relax the jaw, allowing it to release its natural position and reducing the frequency and severity of clenching.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: Lupulone in Humulus Lupulus tea has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the muscles, which can help alleviate tension and clenching in the jaw.

Muscle relaxant effects: The flavonoids and terpenes present in Humulus Lupulus tea have a calming effect on the muscles, including those in the face and jaw, helping to relax tense muscles and reduce clenching.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: The sesquiterpenes found in Humulus Lupulus tea have been shown to have a sedative effect and help reduce anxiety, which can be a common trigger for jaw clenching and bruxism.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used in tea form, Humulus lupulus roots help to relax the muscles and calm tension in the jaw, reducing symptoms of jaw clenching and grinding.

Bark: The bark of the plant contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with temporomandibular joint disorders, making it a common ingredient in teas for jaw clenching relief.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried humulus lupulus hops flowers per cup.
Heat filtered water in a kettle or saucepan and bring it to a rolling boil.
Pour the boiling water over the measured hops flowers in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired flavor intensity.
Strain the tea into cups using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth and serve hot.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw clenching with herbal teas is by regularly drinking chamomile or lemon balm infusions. These teas have calming properties that can help reduce tension in the jaw and promote relaxation. To prepare, pour boiling water over a tea bag or loose leaves and let it steep for several minutes. Drink two to three cups daily, especially before bedtime, to alleviate symptoms. However, if jaw clenching persists or is accompanied by severe pain, consult a healthcare professional for proper evaluation and treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw clenching that are treated with herbal teas include tension headaches and anxiety. Chamomile tea is known for its calming properties and can help reduce tension in the jaw and related headaches. Similarly, lemon balm tea can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help prevent jaw clenching. Passionflower tea is another option that can help reduce anxiety and promote sleep, which can also help alleviate jaw clenching. Peppermint tea can help relax muscles and reduce pain, making it a good choice for treating tension headaches related to jaw clenching. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas as a treatment for any ailment.
